cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what Carmen Rivera wrote the letter to do. The options are (A) order ice-creams, (B) promise to visit again, (C) express her thanks, or (D) give some suggestions. First, I need to understand the main purpose of the letter. The letter starts with Carmen talking about an enjoyable holiday at Magic Kingdom and mentions an event that made their visit magical. The event involves their daughter Gloria and an ice-cream issue on Space Mountain. A staff member named Murphy helped by promising to hold the ice-cream and then replacing it with a new one after the ride. Carmen then says, "We know that ___ tried hard to make our visit special. Thanks so much for going above and beyond!" and signs off as Carmen Rivera. The key part here is the last sentence where she says "Thanks so much for going above and beyond!" which indicates that the purpose of the letter is to express gratitude.
